hints.text: Wordy signs (railway crossing spelled out); US English (center, color)
Yellow centre + white outer lines; **double yellow** common (v. rare in Europe)
White+orange fibre-optic markers ~unique to US; rumble strips common
Trailer/mobile homes rural (esp. SE); Baptist churches common
"No Passing Zone" yellow triangle = US only
US+Canada: post mailbox w/ flag; wooden poles + cylindrical transformers; short plates (no blue strip); overhead signal bars; long-nose semis (Europe = cabover)
Gen2 rare → if Gen2 in N. America likely **Canada** (except Alaska, some Hawaii); Gen3 mirror = single peak (Canada = two peaks)
East of Missouri R. = denser pop. & greener; West = mountains, drier
Deep South (OK→SC) reddish soil; SW Four Corners red mesas/tablelands
PNW (WA/OR/N.CA coast) temperate rainforest; Great Plains flat, few trees
New England architecture (wood panel, porch, high roof) → north
Interstates: **even = W–E, odd = S–N**; lowest numbers south & west
~half of states have **no front plate**; each state has a unique plate design
3-digit area codes (not clustered but learnable); state flags appear
Appalachians (low forested mtns) → east; Rockies → MT–NM; Great Basin (mtns + dry plains) → NV
